name: WHO-vs-CDC growth-standard conflict
description: RF-GRO-002 carries two structurally different, actively-used pediatric growth references — the WHO Child Growth Standards (a prescriptive STANDARD of optimal growth, birth-<24 months) and the CDC 2000 Growth Reference (a descriptive REFERENCE of how a past US population grew, 2-19 years) — plus a mechanical WHO->CDC chart-set switch at 24 months that itself changes screening yield: the CDC 5th/95th-percentile convention flags 7-11% of children 6-23 months as low weight-for-age, while the WHO 2.3rd/97.7th convention (numerically equivalent to +/-2 SD) flags <3% of the SAME population; misapplying the CDC percentile pair to a WHO chart mislabels 10% of an all-healthy reference population. Independently, an abrupt CDC-recommended chart switch at exactly 24 months produces a materially larger mean z-score drop and a higher rate of large single-visit BMIz/weight-z declines (BMIz 28.3% large-drop rate) than a gradual, weighted-average transition chart (11.6%) in the same real-world cohort (n=7429) — i.e. part of any observed "growth faltering" near 24 months is a chart-transition artifact, not a biological change. Per FR-11/FR-12 (seam invariant 8): this is preserved as ONE explicit, named, multi-source conflict object — the WHO and CDC conventions are never averaged, blended into a single cutoff, or silently resolved to whichever source this converter pass happened to read first.
reason: No modules/growth_suite_v1/authoring-decisions.yaml exists (Deferred Item DF-E1-M1) to route this conflict into a rule/candidate draft, and even once one does, seam invariant 8 forbids collapsing a conflict-visible claim set into a single-sided rule — this object is the durable, governance-visible record of the conflict pending that future authoring pass.
